member.name: Steff Aquarone
member.synopsis: Steff Aquarone is the Liberal Democrat MP for North Norfolk , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Equipment: Waste Disposal

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the response given to him on 29 June 2026 to PQ UIN 12919. Defra officials and the Environment Agency (EA) are engaging with Norfolk County Council regarding the challenges it is facing dealing with backlogs of waste electrical ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the commencement of the Register of Overseas Entities regime, up to 31 March 2026, financial penalties have been issued to 445 overseas entities. Of these, 245 penalties with a total value of £13.19 million have been reversed, waived or written off,...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra officials and the Environment Agency (EA) are engaging with Norfolk County Council regarding the challenges it is facing resuming WEEE and batteries recycling services. Local EA teams are providing advice, including advising on Regulatory Position S...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Ownership
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, pursuant to the Answer of 18 June 2026 to Question 9905 on Foreign Companies: Ownership, how many of those penalties were (a) financial sanctions, (b) disqualification and (c) criminal sanctions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Of the 445 penalties issued to overseas entities for failure to register, as of 31 March 2026, all were financial sanctions in the form of civil penalties.Failure to register is a criminal offence under the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Ac...

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what evidence the MCA used to determine changes to coastguard remuneration; and if she will publish that evidence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Changing the Coastguard Rescue Service operating model was not something which we wanted to do but is a mandated consequence of the Court of Appeal ruling. The legal position, the operational implications, the wider organisational impact and the effect on...

writtenQuestions.heading: Primary Education: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will publish new research and guidance on efficiently running small primary schools for small rural primary schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is committed to supporting all schools, including small rural primaries, to manage their resources effectively and maximise value. Through our ‘Maximising value for pupils’ programme, we are helping schools realise better value from existin...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, pursuant to the Answer of 17 June 2026 to Question 11057 on Foreign Companies: Land, what is the total number of land titles bought after 1999 owned by overseas entities which do
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Land Registry does not collect the data in question and so does not hold the information requested. Data showing every completed record about non-UK companies or corporate bodies that own land in England and Wales is available in the dataset of oversea...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Land: Foreign Companies
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what is the total number of land titles bought in England and Wales prior to 1999 which belong to a company or entity based overseas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Before 1999, HM Land Registry did not comprehensively record whether a property was acquired by an overseas entity. For that reason, my Department does not hold the information requested.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what is the total number of Overseas Entities who bought a land title in England and Wales after 1999, that have failed to register on the Register of Overseas Entities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on land titles is held by the relevant UK Land Registries. Based on the data available to Companies House, we are confident that 933 overseas entities are registered proprietors of titles within scope of the Register of Overseas Entities and have fai...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what is the total number land titles bought after 1999 owned by overseas entities which do not have an owner registered on the Register of Overseas Entities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of the latest published figures, over 33,000 overseas entities have registered on the Register of Overseas Entities, indicating strong overall compliance with the regime. Companies House does not hold a definitive figure for the number of land titles b...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Over 33,000 overseas entities have registered on the Register of Overseas Entities, supporting transparency over beneficial ownership. Companies House does not publish or verify figures for the number of overseas entities that have (a) disclosed another e...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cycling: Harassment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what comparative assessment she has made of the levels of cases of abuse and harassment where the victims are cyclists with cases where the victims are (a) drivers and (b) public transport users.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ‘Personal Safety on Public Transport’ survey research was published in February 2026.The findings are based on a survey of 4,900 adults across Great Britain and show the proportion of public transport users who experienced behaviours that undermine pe...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Ownership
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, how many overseas entities which did not comply with beneficial ownership were identified in 2026 to date; how many penalties for non compliance were issued; and how many of those penalties were a) fin
writtenQuestions.answerText: The legislation requires overseas entities to provide details of their managing officers to Companies House when they are unable to identify their beneficial owners. An entity is unable to register or file an update without disclosing this information.Com...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Registration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of removing the £55 fee for requesting trust information on the Register of Overseas Entities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with Managing Public Money (MPM) principles, Companies House sets its fees on a cost recovery basis. The £55 fee for providing access to trust information on the Register of Overseas Entities recovers the cost of delivering the service. Companies ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Companies: Ownership
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, how many overseas entities which did not comply with beneficial ownership were identified in 2025; how many penalties for non compliance were issued; and how many of those penalties were a) financial s
writtenQuestions.answerText: Companies House did not identify any cases of beneficial ownership non-compliance during 2025. The legislation requires overseas entities to either provide details of their beneficial owners, or details of their managing officers when they are unable to i...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Illicit Finance Summit will bring together a wide coalition of country governments and other partners to tackle illicit finance and corruption. As is the normal practice with UK-hosted events, we will publish details of who attended the Summit after i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Crime: Conferences
